concerns.text: FR-001 requires downloading data for ≥100 ASD and ≥100 control participants. T012 only mentions fetching derivatives from OpenNeuro (ds0002800) without specifying the target sample size or the logic to ensure the ≥100 per group threshold is met. The task description lacks the specific requirement to filter/select participants to satisfy the FR-001 volume constraint.

FR-002 (via US-1) and Edge Cases require handling motion exclusion (>3mm). T014 implements this logic but does not explicitly state that the exclusion count must be reported or that the exclusion reason must be logged in the `preprocessing_log.yaml` (which is covered in T016). While T016 covers the log, T014 should explicitly link the exclusion action to the logging requirement to ensure the 'exclusion count' is captured as a metric for SC-001.

FR-009 and SC-004 require a sensitivity analysis sweeping thresholds {10%, 15%, 20%} and reporting the count of nodes significant at all thresholds AND the Jaccard similarity. T025 mentions sweeping thresholds and calculating Jaccard similarity, but omits the explicit requirement to 'report the count of nodes significant at all thresholds'. This missing metric is a core part of the FR-009 deliverable.

FR-010 requires documenting collinearity diagnostics and framing joint relationships descriptively. T026 implements the computation and saving of diagnostics (VIF/correlation matrix) but does not include a task to generate the descriptive framing or the narrative text required by FR-010 ('frame joint relationships descriptively'). The task covers the data output but misses the interpretive requirement.

SC-005 requires reporting classifier performance against a baseline of [deferred] accuracy. T032 saves confidence intervals but does not explicitly mention calculating or reporting the baseline accuracy or class imbalance ratio required by SC-005 to measure performance against. The task needs to ensure the baseline metric is computed and stored.

Constitution Principle V (Versioning Discipline) requires tracking artifact hashes in `state/projects/...yaml`. T009 implements `state_manager.py` to track hashes, but the task description does not explicitly mention that it must track hashes for the specific artifacts defined in the plan (raw, processed, derived) or that it must update the `updated_at` timestamp as required by the Constitution. The task is vague on the specific scope of versioning required.
